steps.title: Find the key signal in the passage
steps.content: The word tension is the defining clue — the passage states that Cannon's work derives its power from the tension among his _____ influences. Tension signals conflict or opposition between forces pulling in different directions. The blank must describe influences that are different enough from each other to create that tension.

steps.title: Eliminate wrong choices and confirm the answer
steps.content:
• Choice A — complementary: Complementary means working together harmoniously — this contradicts the idea of tension, since harmonious influences don't conflict. ✗
  • Choice B — unknown: The influences (portraiture, pop art, flatstyle) are explicitly named in the passage, so calling them unknown is factually wrong. ✗
  • Choice C — disparate: Disparate means fundamentally different or conflicting — portraiture emphasizes realistic depth, pop art emphasizes vivid color, and flatstyle rejects depth entirely, so these influences pull in opposite directions and explain the tension. ✓
  • Choice D — interchangeable: Interchangeable means mutually substitutable — influences that are interchangeable are essentially the same, which cannot produce tension. ✗
That confirms C is correct.
